zh.lifeSummary: 當Annabelle de Burke於1430年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德時，她的父親Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde66歲，母親Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly55歲。她於1440年與King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1466年在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Annabelle de Burke于1430年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德时，她的父亲Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde66岁，母亲Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly55岁。她于1440年与King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1466年在爱尔兰庫迪 高威享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Burton
meanings: English: habitational name from a placename that is very common in central and northern England. The derivation in most cases is from Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
French (northeastern): regional variant of   Berton  .

variants.name: Burkett
meanings: English: from an Old English personal name, Burgheard, composed of the elements burh, burg ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + heard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. The name was reintroduced into Middle English by the Normans in the forms Bou(r)chart, Bocard.

English: possibly also from a Middle English personal name Burcard, Burchard (Norman Burcard, Old French Burchard), which was imported by the Normans and derives from ancient Germanic Burghard (in the form Burkhard this was a very popular medieval German name; see Burkhart ), cognate with the Old English name above. In France this gives rise to the surname Bouchard , Bouchart, but evidence for its use in Norman England is difficult to pin down and modern surnames in Burch- often have a different origin altogether.
English: variant of   Birkett  , a spelling commonly found in northern England.

variants.name: Bourke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Dermot, Liam, Murphy.
Irish: variant of   Burke  .
Americanized form of French   Bourg   or of its altered form   Bourque  .

variants.name: Burgh
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burkeen
meanings: American pet form of Irish   Burke  , not found as a surname in Ireland.
Possibly an altered form of Swiss German Bürgin (see Burgin ).

variants.name: Borke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz.
German (also Börke): from a pet form of a Slavic personal name containing the element bor ‘to fight’.
German (also Börke): topographic name for someone living near a pine forest, from Slavic bor ‘pine tree, fir tree, coniferous forest’.

variants.name: Duxbury
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Duxbury in Lancashire, recorded in the early 13th century as D(e)ukesbiri, from the genitive case of the Old English personal name Deowuc or Duc(c) (both of uncertain origin) + Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ).

variants.name: Borrowman
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian and Dumfriesshire): status name from Middle English burghman, borughman (Old English burhmann) ‘inhabitant of a (fortified) town’ (see Burke ), especially one holding land or buildings by burgage (see Burgess ).

variants.name: Burman
meanings: English: status name, from Middle English burghman, borughman (Old English burhmann) ‘inhabitant of a (fortified) town’ (see Burke ), especially one holding land or buildings by burgage (see Burgess ).
English: variant of   Bowerman  .
Americanized form of German Buhrmann or Bührmann.

nameMeanings.name: Annabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabell
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabella
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Annabelle de Burke was born in 1430, in Clanricarde, County Galway, Ireland, her father, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, was 66 and her mother, Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, was 55. She married King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien in 1440, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1466, in County Galway, Ireland, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Annabelle de Burke est née en 1430, à Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlande, son père, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, avait 66 ans et sa mère, Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, avait 55 ans. Elle a épousé King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien en 1440, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1466, à County Galway, Irlande, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Annabelle de Burke 1430 in Clanricarde, County Galway, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde 66 und ihre Mutter Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly 55. Sie hat 1440 in Irland King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1466 im Alter von 36 Jahren in County Galway, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Annabelle de Burke è nata il nel 1430 nel Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, sua padre, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, aveva 66 anni e sua madre, Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, aveva 55 anni. Lei sposato King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien nel 1440, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1466, nel County Galway, Irlanda, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.Clanricarde, Galway, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ ガルワイクランリカード
ja.Tirconnell, County Donegal, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ フエルマナグティルコネル
ja.Castle Chomhaid, Burren, Thomond, Irelan: アイルランドコウントイ クラーボイリン

ja.lifeSummary: Annabelle de Burkeが1430年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイクランリカードで生まれた時，父Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricardeは66歳，母Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kellyは55歳であった。1440年にアイルランドでKing Tadg an Chomaid O'Brienと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1466年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイで36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Annabelle de Burke(이)가 1430년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 클랜리카드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde(은)는 66세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly(은)는 55세였다. 그녀는 1440년 아일랜드에서 King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1466년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Annabelle de Burke nasceu em 1430, em Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, seu pai, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, tinha 66 anos e sua mãe, Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, tinha 55 anos. Ela casou-se com King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien em 1440, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1466, em County Galway, Irlanda, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Annabelle de Burke родилась в 1430 г. в местности кланрикард, коунти галвеи, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde было 66 лет, а матери, которую звали Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, 55. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien в 1440 г. в местности Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1466 г. в местности коунти галвеи, Ирландия в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Annabelle de Burke nació en 1430, en Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, su papá, Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde, tenía 66 años y su mamá, Fina MacWilliam Burke", "Fina MacWilliam O Kelly" Benomon Deburgh O Kelly, tenía 55 años. Se casó con King Tadg an Chomaid O'Brien en 1440, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1466, en County Galway, Irlanda, a la edad de 36 años.
